whatThisIs: Decode the CMS-1500 professional-claim form field by field. The CMS-1500 (formerly HCFA-1500) is the paper claim form used by non-institutional providers - physicians, NPs, PAs, behavioral health, ambulance, durable-medical-equipment suppliers - to bill commercial and government payers. Sophie Well walks each numbered field from 1 (insurance type) through 33 (billing provider info), explaining what belongs in the box, which fields are required versus situational, and the common pitfalls that cause Box-21 / Box-24 denials.
whenToUse: Use this when a paper claim was returned with a field-error rejection and you need to know exactly which box was wrong, when training a new biller on the layout, or when reconciling an EHR's electronic 837P output against the paper form the payer says it needs. Even when claims are filed electronically, the CMS-1500 field map is the canonical reference - payer companion guides, clearinghouse mapping rules, and denial codes all reference these box numbers, so reading the form natively is a baseline billing-office skill.
